Title: Overlapping and specialized roles of tomato phytoene synthases in carotenoid and abscisic acid production

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carotenoid biosynthesis; carotenoid-derived abscisic acid (ABA) biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: upstream common carotenoid pathway supplying chromoplast carotenoids and ABA precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed and main rate-determining/flux-controlling step of carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two GGPP molecules to produce phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carotenoids function in photoprotection and pigmentation; ABA regulates fruit growth/ripening-related processes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf chloroplasts; flower petal chromoplasts; fruit pericarp chromoplasts; fruit pericarp ABA pool
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: phytoene synthase, plastidial isoprenoid pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: abundant in shoot tissues; strongly expressed in flowers and fruit pericarp, especially after mature green stage; induced by high light in leaves; low in mature seed embryos
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes carotenoid accumulation mainly in flower petals and especially ripe fruit pericarp; also contributes secondarily to leaf photoprotective carotenoids under high light; supplies carotenoid precursors for ABA production in fruit pericarp controlling fruit growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutants (psy1 alleles and genetic combinations with psy2); HPLC pigment profiling; RT-qPCR; chlorophyll fluorescence measurements (ΦPSII, Fv/Fm, NPQ); ABA quantification by UHPLC-MS; fruit weight/volume and germination phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normal light vs high light; fruit developmental stages MG, breaker, B+6; salt stress tested but no root ABA role detected for PSY1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of PSY1 causes paler yellow flowers and pale orange/yellowish ripe fruits with strongly reduced petal xanthophylls and drastically reduced ripe pericarp carotenoids. 2. In leaves, PSY1 contributes modestly to carotenoid accumulation and photoprotection, particularly under high light, but less than PSY2. 3. PSY1-derived carotenoid precursors are specifically required for the major ABA pool in fruit pericarp; psy1 fruits show reduced pericarp ABA, smaller fruit weight and volume, and slower color development.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target to engineer fruit carotenoid content/color and fruit-size traits linked to ABA; stacking with other carotenoid-pathway genes may enhance chromoplast carotenoids, but perturbation may reduce fruit size due to lowered pericarp ABA.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss reduces ripe fruit pigmentation and fruit size/volume; modest reduction in photoprotection under high light.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PSY1 is dispensable for seedling greening if PSY2 is present, but together with PSY2 is essential for shoot carotenoid biosynthesis. Residual carotenoids in psy1 ripe fruit are partly supported by PSY2, especially beta-carotene.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carotenoid biosynthesis; carotenoid-derived abscisic acid (ABA) biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: upstream common carotenoid pathway supplying chloroplast carotenoids and tissue-specific ABA precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed and main rate-determining/flux-controlling step of carotenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two GGPP molecules to produce phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carotenoids function in photoprotection and pigmentation; ABA regulates seed dormancy and salt-stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf chloroplasts; flower petals; minor contribution in fruit pericarp chromoplasts; seeds; salt-stressed roots
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: phytoene synthase, plastidial isoprenoid pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in leaves; induced strongly by high light; present in flowers and seeds; decreases in mature seeds during ripening; low in fruit pericarp relative to PSY1 during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major positive contributor to leaf carotenoid biosynthesis and photoprotection; minor contributor to petal and fruit chromoplast carotenoids; principal source of carotenoid precursors for ABA synthesis in seeds and salt-stressed roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout mutants (psy2 alleles and genetic combinations with psy1); HPLC pigment profiling; chlorophyll fluorescence measurements (ΦPSII, Fv/Fm, NPQ); ABA quantification by UHPLC-MS; seed germination assays; inhibitor treatments with fosmidomycin and norflurazon
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high light; fruit developmental stages; salt stress (200 mM NaCl, 24 h); inhibitor treatment of mature green fruits with fosmidomycin or norflurazon
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY2 is the predominant isoform supporting carotenoid biosynthesis in leaf chloroplasts and photoprotection, as psy2 mutants show reduced carotenoids, lower ΦPSII/Fv/Fm, and lower NPQ, especially under high light. 2. PSY2 contributes only modestly to petal and ripe fruit carotenoids, but supports residual beta-carotene accumulation in PSY1-deficient fruit. 3. PSY2 is the main isoform linked to ABA production in seeds and salt-stressed roots; psy2 seeds have reduced ABA and accelerated germination, and psy2 roots fail to increase ABA under salt stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving leaf photoprotection and controlling seed dormancy or stress-responsive ABA production; manipulation should consider possible impacts on germination behavior and stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs photoprotection and photosynthetic efficiency, accelerates seed germination due to lower seed ABA, and compromises root ABA response to salt stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PSY2 is not sufficient alone to prevent albino lethality when both PSY1 and PSY2 are absent; together with PSY1 it is essential for shoot carotenoid production. Seed dormancy specifically depends on ABA made in mature seeds from PSY2-derived carotenoids.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not directly established in this study
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses two GGPP molecules to produce phytoene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports root-associated apocarotenoid functions, particularly arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis context as discussed
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: phytoene synthase, plastidial isoprenoid pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: hardly detectable in shoot tissues; highest in roots; induced during arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization according to cited work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: does not contribute detectably to carotenoid biosynthesis in shoot tissues, and does not substantially contribute to salt-induced root ABA synthesis under the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic inference from double psy1 psy2 albino seedlings retaining functional PSY3; RT-qPCR expression analysis; ABA analysis in salt-stressed roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: shoot development; high light leaves; salt stress roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Functional PSY3 cannot compensate for simultaneous loss of PSY1 and PSY2 in shoot tissues, as double mutants are albino and seedling lethal. 2. PSY3 transcripts are essentially absent from shoots. 3. Salt-induced root ABA production is attributed to PSY2 rather than PSY3 under the tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value from this paper for fruit/leaf nutritional engineering; may be relevant for root/apocarotenoid traits such as mycorrhization based on prior work.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PSY3 is discussed as a root-specialized isoform, but it is not a primary experimental target with direct mutant analysis in this study.
